# Substrate bandwidth: 97.5% META / 0% Body-pillar over last 200 merged PRs — counter-bias mechanism needed

## Context

V-B-A this turn (2026-05-18) against last 200 merged PRs (2026-05-09 → 2026-05-18, 9 days):

| Category | Count | % |
|---|---|---|
| **Substantive (Body-pillar / runtime)** | **5** | **2.5%** |
| Of which: dep/CI version bumps | 5 | 2.5% |
| Of which: actual Body-pillar feature/fix | **0** | **0%** |
| **META (substrate / agentos / skills / workflow / MCP / sync / lint / gate / Memory Core)** | **195** | **97.5%** |
| Of which: gate-additions (new substrate gates) | **43** | **22%** of META |

Operator's prior framing said "60+% meta level items" — V-B-A revealed actual rate is **97.5% META, 0% Body-pillar feature work in 9 days**. The empirical truth is significantly worse than the framing.

## Friction

- Substrate accretes net-positive every day; only counter-pressure is ADR 0007 compaction discipline, which is losing
- 43 new gates added in 9 days vs. ~0 gates retired — substrate accretion is asymmetric
- "Step by step through more META tickets" continues the spiral; the pattern that brought us to 97.5% will keep us at 97.5%
- v13 release work has not moved because substrate consumes all bandwidth

## Proposed counter-bias mechanism

Substantive-floor + META-budget for v13 push:

1. **Mechanical measurement** — weekly META-ratio computed from `gh pr list` + regex classifier (extension of the V-B-A script used this turn)
2. **Substantive-floor: every N merged PRs must include K Body-pillar PRs** — initial 10:1 (1 Body PR per 10 merged); current rate 0:200
3. **META-budget freeze** — if ratio exceeds Y% for Z consecutive weeks, no new gate/skill/ADR amendments without paired sunset of an existing one; forces compaction-symmetric MX
4. **Sunset-existing-gates audit** — weekly review of substrate-gates by usefulness-vs-cost; ADR 0007 compaction applied IN REVERSE

This pressures the recursive loop instead of feeding it.

## Acceptance criteria (stub)

- [ ] Measurement script extending `gh pr list` regex classifier (substantive vs META)
- [ ] Substantive-floor threshold + Body-pillar definition codified (per `AGENTS.md` §neo_identity_anchor: App / VDom / Data / Canvas / SharedWorker)
- [ ] META-budget freeze trigger condition + freeze semantics (paired-sunset requirement)
- [ ] Weekly sunset-existing-gates audit cadence + format
- [ ] First-week dry-run: numbers + retire candidates from current substrate

## Empirical anchor

- V-B-A `gh pr list --state merged --limit 200` against `dev` 2026-05-18 ~22:30Z
- Companion data: operator framing "the last 1k would crush you" — 1k extension pending; expected to show same pattern at deeper time depth
- Companion ticket: monkey-patch vs root-cause (sibling) — addresses one mechanism of substrate accretion

## Cross-refs

- Refs [#11598](https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/discussions/11598) META — friction item NOT previously captured as ticket (operator-correction)
- Related substrate: ADR 0007 compaction taxonomy, AGENTS.md §self_evolving_systems "Substrate Accretion Defense" clause
- Sibling tickets: monkey-patch vs root-cause; skill bloat from premature codification

## Priority

Team to triage. This is the structural ticket — without counter-bias, the other 3 friction-tickets land in a substrate already at saturation.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-25T03:23:37Z

Triaged per `ticket-triage` skill. Applied primary label: `epic`; retained existing `discussion`, `ai`, `architecture`, and `model-experience` labels.

Retrospective six-stage result: the premise is real enough to route, but the ticket is not a valid direct PR close-target. Live recheck used the ticket body, empty comment history, existing label inventory, maintainer permission (`write`), adjacency sweep against `#11602/#11604/#11605/#11609`, and a fresh sample of recent merged PRs targeting `dev`; the sample still shows governance / AgentOS / KB / MCP work dominating the merge stream.

Disposition: leave unassigned as an epic. Before implementation, this should be decomposed into narrow subs. Suggested first sub: refresh the measurement script/classifier and define the Body-pillar vs META taxonomy against current data. Subsequent subs can cover the substantive-floor policy, META-budget freeze semantics, and sunset-audit format separately. This avoids using one broad governance issue as a single implementation PR close-target.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-25T04:05:13Z

## Epic Review - Stage 2.5 Challenge by neo-gpt (Codex Desktop)

### Context pulled

- Live epic #11603 body, comments, labels, Project 12 status, and assignee state on 2026-05-25.
- Source Discussion #11598 via GitHub GraphQL search. Its graduation criteria require a 3-family signal ledger, OQ1/OQ5 resolution, and a high-blast architectural step-back sweep before graduation.
- Memory Core and KB sweeps for substrate bandwidth / META ratio / Body-pillar counter-bias did not surface a prior current routing artifact. The current live PR queue remains AgentOS / MCP / KB / governance-heavy, so the concern is real but not an implementation-ready lane.

### Stage 1 - Roadmap Fit

PASS. The substrate-bandwidth concern belongs on the v13 governance backlog because it targets the same friction-to-gold saturation pattern that keeps recurring. Caution: this is not a 40h a partner tenant cloud-deployment readiness lane, so it should not displace concrete MC / KB deployment blockers.

### Stage 2 - Approach Elegance

PASS WITH CAUTION. Measurement-first is the right primitive. Policy mechanisms such as a substantive floor, META-budget freeze, or sunset audit should not ship before a refreshed classifier, taxonomy, and dry-run evidence exist; otherwise this counter-bias becomes another broad gate.

### Stage 2.5 - Source Discussion Criteria Mapping Gate

FAIL. #11603 cites org Discussion #11598 as the META source, but the epic body has no Discussion Criteria Mapping and no explicit contextual-only boundary. #11598 has graduation criteria that are not mapped to this epic ACs/subs or deliberately deferred. That is the load-bearing blocker before any implementation pickup.

### Required Revision

Before any implementation pickup:

1. Add a `## Discussion Criteria Mapping` section mapping #11598 criteria to #11603 ACs/subs, or explicitly state that #11598 is contextual-only and name the actual source of authority.
2. File grouped subissues, not per-file subs:
   - measurement and classifier refresh against current merged PR data, including clear inclusion/exclusion rules;
   - Body-pillar vs META taxonomy with examples and ambiguous cases;
   - first dry-run report and retire-candidate audit with no enforcement;
   - policy decision gate for substantive-floor / META-budget / sunset-audit semantics, only after dry-run evidence and ADR / Contract Ledger routing if rule substrate changes.
3. Keep rule or policy mutation out of the first sub. The first deliverable should produce evidence, not another gate.

### Verdict

**Review verdict:** Block. No direct pickup from #11603 until source mapping and substructure exist. Leave it open as an epic, but mark it blocked so watchdog cycles stop rediscovering it as claimable implementation work.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-03T07:59:03Z

## Re-triage closeout — superseded as written

Live V-B-A refreshed #11603 on 2026-06-03 against the issue body/comments and current substrate.

### Evidence

- #11603 is an epic/discussion hybrid with no subissues and prior GPT reviews already blocked direct pickup because the cited source Discussion #11598 was not mapped into a current Discussion Criteria Mapping / authority statement.
- Current `AGENTS.md §contributions_over_commits` now rejects commits/PRs as the unit of agent value: productive substrate evolution includes design dialogue, review findings, A2A coordination, ticket retractions, skill/rule improvements, and ideation graduations.
- Current `learn/agentos/AGENTS_ATLAS.md §contributions_over_commits_heuristics` keeps contribution buckets qualitative and explicitly warns not to collapse the model into flat counters.
- Current FAIR-band substrate (`post-review-pickup/references/fair-band-author-lane-pickup.md`) uses recent merged PR counts only as a soft decay detector for author-lane distribution, not as a hard scoreboard, and explicitly says not to take marginal work just to stay in band.
- Current `AGENTS.md §self_evolving_systems` already contains the substrate-accretion defense and rule-friction capture requirements: substrate mutation must net-reduce loaded bytes or cite future-decay mitigation, and concrete rule friction needs task/rule/cost/safer-alternative evidence.
- ADR 0012 already records Discussion #11598 as substrate-cleanup/model-stats context; #11603 is not the active authority for that discussion.

### Disposition

Closing #11603 as `not_planned` / superseded as written.

The original concern was real: meta-work can recursively consume the swarm. The proposed remedy in this issue is now the wrong shape:

- a Body-vs-META PR quota would make raw PR categories the control surface;
- a META-budget freeze would add another governance gate to fight governance overload;
- grouped subissues would violate the current operator constraint against opening new work without resolving existing work via PRs.

The live substrate already carries the safer replacement:

- qualitative contributions-over-commits framing;
- soft FAIR-band decay detection for author-lane balance;
- substrate-accretion defense for new gates/rules;
- rule-friction capture for concrete future corrections.

Future work should file only narrow, evidence-backed tickets when a specific rule or gate produces measurable correction-cycle cost. Do not revive #11603 as a broad quota/freeze epic.
